readme.excerpt:
# Nuclide

[![Circle Status](https://circleci.com/gh/facebook/nuclide/tree/master.svg?style=shield&circle-token=a805ec51c07ae99799ce548795383dd1f1e4dc76)](https://circleci.com/gh/facebook/nuclide)

[Nuclide](http://nuclide.io/) is a collection of features for [Atom](https://atom.io/) to provide IDE-like functionality for a variety of programming languages and technologies.

The [Nuclide license](https://github.com/facebook/nuclide/blob/master/LICENSE) has certain limitations around distribution and should not be considered an [open source license](https://opensource.org/licenses/alphabetical). However, this does not affect your ability to [fork the project and make contributions](https://github.com/facebook/nuclide/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

---

## Documentation

The [full documentation](http://nuclide.io/docs) for Nuclide provides information for:

- [Client Installation](http://nuclide.io/docs/editor/setup) ([Mac](http://nuclide.io/docs/editor/setup/#mac) | [Linux](http://nuclide.io/docs/editor/setup/#linux) | [Windows is not supported](http://nuclide.io/docs/editor/setup/#windows))
- [Server Installation](http://nuclide.io/docs/editor/setup/#installing-nuclide-server)
- [Getting Started Feature Walkthrough](http://nuclide.io/docs/quick-start/getting-started/)
- [Building from Source](http://nuclide.io/docs/advanced-topics/building-from-source/)

death.reason: Repository was archived by its owner.

autopsy.epitaph: Built atop Atom's shoulders to illuminate a thousand codebases, Nuclide was quietly interred in December 2018 when Facebook archived it, its final breath a typo-laden update to a landing page announcing its own deprecation.

causes.label: Deliberate abandonment by Facebook — archived by owner
Atom platform dependency made standalone survival impossible
Chronic unresolved bug accumulation and zero maintainer response
Facebook-internal module leakage rotting the OSS build

revival.step: Fork the repository under a neutral org, strip all fb-internal module references (e.g. fb-appsession-observer, fb-highlight-selected) and replace with open stubs or remove entirely
Migrate from the dead Atom editor platform to a VS Code extension host or a standalone Electron shell, preserving the remote-development and Flow integration features
Triage and close the 59 open issues, writing a clear deprecation/migration notice for each category of bug so the community understands what is and isn't being fixed
Establish a CI pipeline (the CircleCI badge in the README already existed) and wire it to the forked repo so pull requests can be validated automatically
Publish the revived package under a new name to avoid confusion with the archived Facebook release, and write a migration guide from the old nuclide.io documentation

autopsy.lastWordsGloss: The final commit corrected the world that Nuclide was dying — misspelling 'deprecation' as 'depracation' in the process, because even the eulogy needed a bug fix.
